title: Use of an esterase to enhance ethyl ester content in fermentation media
abstract: Methods to prepare vegetable oil compositions having an elevated ethyl ester content are provided.
claims: Claims IS CLAI 1. A method for enhancing corn oil properties from ground com subjected to fermentation, comprising: providing an aqueous composition comprising ground com and a glucoamylase under conditions which produce glucose; and fermenting the glucose in the presence of a yeast under conditions which produce ethanol and com oil having an ethyl ester content that is greater than 7% w/w based on the total weight of the composition, wherein an amount of an exogenous esterase is added to the composition before fermentation, is added during fermentation or is added after fermentation, or any combination thereof. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the ethyl ester content is greater than about 10% w/w in the total weight of the oil composition. 3. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the ethyl ester content is greater than about 30% w/w in the total weight of the oil composition. 4. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the ethyl ester content is greater than about 50% w/w in the total weight of the oil composition. 5. The method of claim 1 or 2, wherein the ethyl ester content is greater than about 60% w/w in the total weight of the composition. 6. The method of any one of claims 1 to 5, wherein the esterase is a lipase. 7. The method of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the esterase is in an amount that is at least 0.01 % w/w of the weight of corn fat in the aqueous composition prior to fermentation. 8. The method of any one of claims 1 to 6, wherein the esterase is in an amount that is at least 0.4 % w/w of the weight of corn fat in the aqueous composition prior to fermentation. 9. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the esterase is in an amount that is at least 0.01 % w/w of the weight of corn fat after fermentation. 10. The method of any one of claims 1 to 8, wherein the esterase is in an amount that is at least 0.4 % w/w of the weight of corn fat after fermentation. 11. The method of any one of claims 1 to 10, wherein more than 50% of the ground corn passes through a 0.5 mm screen. 12. The method of any one of claims I to 11, wherein the aqueous composition further comprises an amount of fungal acid amylase. 13. The method of claim 12, wherein the amount of the fungal acid amylase ranges from about 0.1 to about 10 fungal acid amylase units per gram of said dry solids, and the amount of the glucoamylase to dry solids in said composition which ranges from about 0.5 to about 6 glucoamylase units per gram of said dry solids. 14. The method of any one of claims 1 to 13, wherein during the production of ethanol, the pH is maintained at 3-4.5 during the first half of the fill cycle and at 4.5-6.0 during the second half of the fill cycle, 15. The method of any one of claims 1 to 14, wherein the conditions include a pH of from 3 to 6, a temperature of from about 25Â°C to about 40Â°C and a solids content in said composition of from about 20 to 50 weight percent. 16. The method of any one of claims 1 to 15, wherein the conditions include a pH of from about 3 to 6, maintaining a glucose concentration in the aqueous composition of less than about 2 weight percent after 12 hours of saccharification and fermentation, or both. 17. The method of any one of claims I to 16, wherein said method produces at least 18 volume percent ethanol. 18. The method of any one of claims 1 to 17, wherein glucose is produced at a temperature of from about 30Â° C to about 35Â°C, and/or a solids content in the composition is from about 25 to 45 weight percent 19. The method of any one of claims 1 to 18, wherein the glucose is fermented under conditions comprising an initial temperature of about 35Â°C which temperature is decreased during fermentation to a temperature of about 30Â°C, and/or maintaining a glucose concentration in the aqueous composition of less than about I weight percent after 12 hours of saccharification and fermentation, wherein the production of glucose and the fermentation of glucose to ethanol is conducted simultaneously. 20. The method of any one of claims 1 to 19, wherein the esterase is a bacterial, yeast, plant or fungal esterase. 21. The method of any one of claims 1 to 20, wherein the com oil further comprises a lutein content of at least 50 mcg/g, a zeaxanthin content of at least 30 mcg/g, a cis-Iutein/zeaxanthin content of at least 10 mcg/g, an alpha- cryptoxanthin content of at least 5 mcg/g, a beta-cryptoxanthin content of at least 5 mcg/g, an alpha-carotene content of at least 0.5 mcg/g, a beta-carotene content of at least 1 mcg/g, a cis-beta-carotene content of at least 0.1 mcg/g, an alpha- tocopherol content of at least 50 mcg/g, a beta- tocopherol content of at least 2 mcg/g, a gamma-tocopherol content of at least 300 mcg/g, a delta-tocopherol content of at least 15 mcg/g, an alpha-tocotrienol content of at least 50 mcg/g, a beta-tocotrienol content of at least 5 mcg/g, a gamma-tocotrienol content of at least 80 mcg/g, a delta- tocotrienol content of at least 5 mcg/g, or any combination thereof.
